title: sticherus pruinosus
intro: Sticherus pruinosus, also known as the hairy fork fern, is a type of fern native to New Caledonia. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its saponins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Sticherus pruinosus include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Sticherus pruinosus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile and accessible natural remedy.
